hook.text: A hospital requires one doctor on call. Two doctors check, see the other is on call, and both sign off. Nobody's left.

buildup.text: Write skew: two transactions read the same data, decide, and write different rows that together break an invariant.

discovery.text: Read Committed and Repeatable Read don't prevent write skew. Only Serializable does.

twist.text: Write skew is subtle. The individual transactions look correct. The problem only appears when both commit.

climax.text: Fix: use SELECT FOR UPDATE on the rows being checked, or switch to Serializable isolation for that path.

punchline.text: Write skew: valid alone, broken together. Serialize the critical path.

quiz.question: Why is write skew hard to detect in testing?
options: Each transaction looks correct individually; the problem only appears when both run concurrently
Write skew only occurs in production environments
Testing tools automatically prevent write skew
